findings.summary: RenameRelative allocates structureSize (24 on 64-bit, 16 on 32-bit) plus name.Length bytes. The FILE_RENAME_INFO header ends at nameOffset=20 on 64-bit, so the buffer is 4 bytes larger than the strict minimum. This is safe; the comment referencing the minimum-size contract is correct.

In PublishAll's catch handler, CleanupUnpublished is invoked before re-throwing. If CleanupUnpublished itself throws (e.g., FileDispositionInfo set fails), the original rename exception is replaced by the cleanup exception, which may complicate post-failure diagnosis. This does not affect the security or correctness of published files and is an accepted operational trade-off.

Get-RepositoryRoot performs path-based filesystem traversal before AnchoredCopySession is constructed. This pre-anchor traversal cannot be fully avoided without a caller-supplied root; the -RepositoryRoot parameter provides that escape. All security-critical copy, hash, and rename paths operate exclusively through the anchored session.

The staged MANIFEST.sha256 contains exactly 18 entries in inventory order with lowercase-64-hex digests separated by two spaces, matching the Get-ManifestDigests parser contract and the phase2_human_copy_targets inventory declared in requirements.md.

The native rename API addendum records that SetFileInformationByHandle returns Win32 87 on the reviewed PS5.1/NTFS host when RootDirectory is non-null, triggering NtSetInformationFile with the same handles, leaf buffer, and replacement flag. Any other error or NtSetInformationFile failure aborts before post-install verification; no path-based fallback exists. ADR 0011 and the human decision of 2026-07-14 authorize this specific fallback.

TEST_FIXTURE_SOURCE_SUBSTITUTION, TEST_FIXTURE_PARENT_SUBSTITUTION, TEST_FIXTURE_AFTER_PREPARE_ITEM, and TEST_FIXTURE_BEFORE_RENAME comment markers are present in the C# source at the expected injection points, consistent with the isolated fixture-only instrumented runner contract required by TEST-013.

RED evidence (55 passed / 13 failed) maps cleanly to the 13 expected failing contracts listed in the critical preflight table. GREEN evidence (68/68 PowerShell, 33/33 Bash, generator --check pass, workflow-state ok) satisfies AC-013 and the regression rerun of TEST-001 through TEST-004. The security review confirms no credential, token, or API-key literals in staged artifacts.
